title: holosteum umbellatum subsp. glutinosum
intro: Holosteum umbellatum subsp. glutinosum, also known as sticky mouse-ear, is a subspecies of the Holosteum umbellatum plant, a flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Holosteum umbellatum subsp. glutinosum can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
